fix(lxst): resolve audio choppiness with output pacing and jitter buffer

Root cause was burst-drain oscillation: when frames arrived in bursts,
digestJob drained them faster than real-time (writeAudio returns instantly
when AudioTrack buffer has room after underrun), causing queue starvation
and repeated underruns.

Fixes:
- LineSink: pace output at frame rate via delay() after fast writes,
  preventing burst-drain. Pre-fill 5 frames (AUTOSTART_MIN=5) before
  playback. Async start() to avoid blocking Mixer. Never stop AudioTrack
  on underrun (avoids 880ms rebuild gap). Poll timeout = frameTimeMs.
- KotlinAudioBridge: 500ms AudioTrack buffer floor for jitter absorption.
  Removed per-frame diagnostic scanning from writeAudio/recordingLoop.
- Mixer: moved mixing computation outside synchronized(insertLock) to
  avoid blocking LinkSource frame delivery.
- LinkSource: added throughput counter for RX diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(lxst): extract lxst module with audio pipeline, codecs, and telephony

Extract the LXST (voice call) stack into a standalone Gradle module
(lxst/) decoupled from the reticulum module. Includes the full audio
pipeline (LineSink, LineSource, Mixer, Packetizer, Pipeline), codec
wrappers (Opus with 16kHz decode-rate cap for wuqi-opus 1024-sample
limit, Codec2), bridge layer (KotlinAudioBridge with 500ms AudioTrack
buffer floor, NetworkPacketBridge, CallBridge), and telephony state
machine (Telephone, Profile, NetworkTransport interface).

LineSink improvements:
- Monotonic clock pacer eliminates cumulative drift from relative delays
- Post-underrun re-buffering (AUTOSTART_MIN frames) prevents rapid
  micro-underrun cycles that caused audible pops
- Underrun duration threshold: brief glitches (<2 frame periods) resume
  immediately, sustained gaps trigger full re-buffer
- MAX_FRAMES increased to 15 (900ms jitter absorption at 60ms/frame)

Test coverage:
- LineSinkPacingTest: 9 unit tests for monotonic clock, burst pacing,
  drift, underrun recovery, re-buffer behavior
- LineSinkRebufferInstrumentedTest: 5 hardware tests validating
  re-buffer strategy against real AudioTrack underrun counts
- OpusDecodeRateInstrumentedTest: 8 tests documenting wuqi-opus
  1024-sample buffer constraint and 16kHz decode cap
- LineSinkPacingInstrumentedTest, FullDuplexInstrumentedTest,
  LineSourceCaptureInstrumentedTest: hardware validation suite

Known limitations:
- RX delayed ~2-3s (16kHz decode → 60ms frames → large pre-buffer)
- Occasional TX pops (separate from RX re-buffer work)
- Fix requires replacing wuqi-opus with caller-allocated-buffer library

fix(lxst): replace wuqi-opus with libopus-android for native 48kHz decode

wuqi-opus hardcoded a 1024-sample output buffer in native code, forcing
RX decode to cap at 16kHz (960 samples/frame ≤ 1024). This caused
audible quality loss (8kHz Nyquist limit vs 24kHz at native 48kHz).

Switch to libopus-android (already vendored at external/codec2_talkie/)
which uses caller-allocated buffers with no size limit. VOICE_HIGH now
decodes at native 48kHz (2880 samples for 60ms frames).

Changes:
- Swap Maven dependency to project(:external:codec2_talkie:libopus-android)
- Rewrite Opus.kt for libopus-android API (single native handle, lazy init)
- Remove computeSafeDecodeSamplerate() and all wuqi-opus workarounds
- Remove actualDecodeSamplerate from Telephone.kt (use preferredSamplerate)
- Remove hardcoded NARROWBAND/VOICE from OpusJNI.cpp (auto-select)
- Remove stale overrideLibrary="com.theeasiestway.opus" from manifests
- Rewrite OpusDecodeRateInstrumentedTest for native-rate verification
- Update LineSinkRebufferInstrumentedTest comments

Tested: crystal clear bidirectional audio in Sideband ↔ Columba call,
all 8 instrumented tests pass, all unit tests pass.

fix(lxst): enable SHQ stereo profile and drain startup buffering delay

SHQ (PROFILE_VOICE_MAX) is the only stereo voice profile (48kHz, 2ch).
Two bugs prevented it from working:

TX: LineSource captures mono, but Opus stereo codec calculated frame
duration as 30ms (2880 samples / 96000) instead of 60ms, throwing
CodecError and silently dropping every frame. Added mono-to-stereo
upmix in Opus.encode() matching Python LXST Opus.py:113-122.

RX: LinkSource.channels and LineSink were hardcoded to mono, garbling
stereo decode output. Telephone.openPipelines() now propagates
decodeCodec.codecChannels through the RX pipeline.

Startup delay: AudioTrack creation takes ~500ms, during which frames
accumulate to ~13 (780ms). Added drain after startPlayback() to
discard stale frames, keeping only AUTOSTART_MIN (5) most recent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lxst): fix Codec2 bit-packing destroying 7/8 of encoded audio data

The codec2_talkie JNI library's getBitsSize() returns bytes per frame,
not bits (confirmed by their source: "// returns number of bytes").
Our code treated it as a bit count, computing bytesPerFrame=(8+7)/8=1
instead of bytesPerFrame=8, then bit-packed 8 bytes into 1 byte on
encode and unpacked 1 byte to 8 bit-values on decode. This destroyed
nearly all encoded information, causing severe audio artifacts.

Fix: use getBitsSize() directly as bytesPerFrame, copy CharArray to
ByteArray without bit manipulation on encode, pass ByteArray directly
to JNI decode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(app): restore Columba launcher icon overridden by codec library

External codec library modules (libcodec2-android, libopus-android) included
default Android Studio launcher icons. AAPT2 compiled the library's

drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml due to qualifier precedence on API 24+.

Place the app's Columba constellation icon at both drawable-v24 and
drawable-anydpi-v24 to ensure it wins at every qualifier level.

fix(lxst): time-based buffering, lenient re-buffer threshold, crash recovery

Three fixes for LineSink audio playback:

1. Time-based buffer sizing: BUFFER_CAPACITY_MS (1500ms) and PREBUFFER_MS
   (500ms) replace fixed frame counts, giving all profiles adequate jitter
   absorption (MQ 25 frames, LL 75, ULL 150).

2. Lenient re-buffer: REBUFFER_TRIGGER_MS (500ms) replaces frameTimeMs*2
   (40ms). Brief jitter gaps resume immediately letting AudioTrack's
   internal buffer absorb them. Only sustained outages re-buffer to
   REBUFFER_FRAMES (5). Eliminates ~7.6s silence from 6 false re-buffer
   events in a 14.6s call on high-jitter Reticulum links.

3. Crash recovery: try-finally in start() resets isRunningFlag on
   digestJob crash, allowing autostart to recover instead of permanent
   silence.

feat(lxst): replace GPL-2.0 codec2_talkie JNI wrappers with MIT-licensed equivalents

Replace the GPL-2.0 licensed codec2_talkie git submodule (sh123/codec2_talkie)
with clean-room MIT-licensed C JNI wrappers that link directly to the prebuilt
LGPL-2.1 libcodec2.so and BSD-3-Clause libopus.so libraries.

New JNI wrappers (lxst_codec2_jni.c, lxst_opus_jni.c) implement only the 10
functions lxst uses (vs 31 in the GPL wrapper) and fix several bugs:
- Use opus_encoder_destroy()/opus_decoder_destroy() instead of free()
- Remove redundant opus_encoder_init() after opus_encoder_create()
- Encode Codec2 directly to ByteArray (eliminates CharArray conversion quirk)
- Use uintptr_t for handle casts (safe on both ILP32 and LP64)

Also fix pre-existing CodecRoundTripTest bitrate ceiling tests that were never
validated on device: test average bitrate over 50 frames instead of asserting
per-frame compliance (Opus VBR is a long-term average, not per-frame guarantee),
and always feed mono input to stereo profiles (encode() handles upmix internally).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lxst): stop LineSink and ringerSink on call teardown

stopPipelines() stopped all pipeline components except audioOutput
(LineSink), and stopRingTone() omitted ringerSink. The orphaned
LineSink digestJob coroutine kept running after hangup, holding
KotlinAudioBridge.isPlaying=true. On the next call, the new LineSink's
bridge.startPlayback() returned immediately ("already started") without
creating a fresh AudioTrack, causing no audio on the callee's second
and subsequent calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(lxst): speaker toggle via AudioTrack recreation, jitter-absorbing playback

Speaker toggle: replace ineffective setPreferredDevice() per-track hint
with full AudioTrack stop/recreate cycle. Many HALs (Samsung Fold3, some
Pixels) don't dynamically re-route an already-playing AudioTrack when
setCommunicationDevice() changes. A fresh AudioTrack inherits routing
state at creation time, making the toggle reliable on all tested devices.

Playback jitter: remove explicit monotonic-clock pacing delay from
LineSink digestJob. The old pacer kept the AudioTrack buffer at ~1 frame
(60ms), so any network jitter >60ms caused an audible pop. Now
WRITE_BLOCKING on AudioTrack provides natural pacing — it blocks when
the buffer is full and returns when hardware frees space. The prebuffer
(480ms for MQ) fills the AudioTrack buffer upfront, absorbing network
jitter up to ~480ms without audible interruption.

Confirmed: zero LineSink underruns on both Galaxy Z Fold3 and S21 Ultra
(previously 24 underruns per 20s call on the S21 Ultra).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(python): guard call() with _busy flag to prevent race with incoming calls

The call() method checked and set self.active_call without holding
_call_handler_lock. During the up-to-70s path discovery window, an
incoming call could set active_call via __caller_identified, which
call() would then overwrite — orphaning the incoming link.

Fix: set _busy=True under lock before path discovery so incoming
calls see the reservation and get rejected. Clear _busy in finally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(telephony): improve call setup reliability and init speed

- Announce lxst.telephony destination alongside LXMF announces so
  remote peers discover both messaging and call paths together
- Add periodic re-announcing in CallManager (3-hour interval,
  matching reference LXST Telephony implementation)
- Auto-request identity path from network when unknown, with 5s
  retry loop (mirrors existing LXMF pattern in reticulum_wrapper)
- Move setupLxstCallManager() to first position in setupBridges()
  so Telephone is available immediately after Python init (was last,
  causing up to ~47s delay before calls could be placed)
- Remove stale "call.audio" announce handler that could never match
  the actual "lxst.telephony" aspect
